author: Subramanyam6
name: 11L XSA + SVD-BigramSystem + AdamW v_t Saliency-Weighted GPTQ
blurb: Non-record submission demonstrating AdamW v_t Saliency-Weighted GPTQ: uses optimizer second moments (EMA of grad²) to boost Hessian diagonal for high-saliency weights during int6 quantization. Also: SVD-initialized BigramSystem with GPU-native index_add_. Does not beat SOTA due to bytes_per_token bug (hardcoded 3.5 vs actual 2.436) and 10× training-speed gap (no Flash Attention 3, ~700 steps vs SOTA's 6922). Corrected post-GPTQ BPB: 1.488 (seed=314). Architecture is sound; see README for fix plan.
val_bpb_note: Corrected using bytes_per_token=2.436 (measured from sp1024 on FineWeb). Raw bits/token=3.6251, BPB=3.6251/2.436=1.488.
val_bpb_inflated_note: Our training script reported 1.036 BPB using wrong bytes_per_token=3.5. All in-training checkpoints are similarly inflated.

314.note: Official post-GPTQ eval: bits_per_token=3.6251, bytes_per_token=2.436, BPB=1.488

novel_contributions: AdamW v_t (exp_avg_sq) saliency boosting of GPTQ Hessian diagonal — uses optimizer state to identify high-gradient-pressure weights
SVD-initialized BigramSystem — initializes bigram embeddings via truncated SVD of empirical co-occurrence matrix
GPU-native BigramSystem population via CUDA index_add_ (avoids numpy bottleneck)
known_issues: bytes_per_token hardcoded to 3.5 instead of measuring from tokenizer (actual: 2.436 for sp1024 on FineWeb)
No Flash Attention 3: only ~700 gradient steps in 600s vs SOTA's 6922 steps
fp32 softmax for XSA numerical stability adds significant compute overhead
grad_accum=4 adds overhead vs grad_accum=1 in SOTA
calibration: AR self-generated (32 seqs x 1024 tokens, fixed seed)
technique_summary: AdamW v_t Saliency-Weighted GPTQ int6 + SVD-BigramSystem + XSA all-layers + EMA(0.997) + Parallel Muon + zstd-22
